steps.title: Identify the comparative structure
steps.content: The passage shows a limitation (works presenting method without motive) and then pivots with “However” to a more complete approach (works providing both method and motive). This problem-then-better-solution pattern reveals a comparison of which works are more comprehensive.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content: A describes this structure precisely: the passage characterizes moral philosophy through two example works and then details how some works in that field are more comprehensive than others by including both method and motive. B, C, and D each misread the passage—either overstating the problem as impossible to overcome, fabricating a research proposal, or inventing a readership comparison. This matches choice A — A is correct.
